author: skyline75489
text: [1.9 RC1] CMake failed to correctly link torch & c10 libraries on Windows

author: skyline75489
text:
I'm using CMake with Visual Studio with this `CMakeLists.txt`:

```
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.5 FATAL_ERROR)
project(TestProject)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 14)

list(APPEND CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH "D:/Lib/libtorch-win-shared-with-deps-1.9.0-rc1/libtorch")

find_package(torch REQUIRED)
add_executable(TestProject ${SOURCES})

target_compile_features(TestProject PUBLIC cxx_range_for)
target_link_libraries(TestProject ${TORCH_LIBRARIES})
```

This is what `build.ninja` looks like with 1.8.1:

```
#############################################
# Link the executable TestProject.exe
build TestProject.exe: CXX_EXECUTABLE_LINKER__TestProject_RelWithDebInfo CMakeFiles\TestProject.dir\LibTorchTestApp.cpp.obj | 
D$:\Lib\libtorch-win-shared-with-deps-1.8.1+cu102\libtorch\lib\torch.lib D$:\Lib\libtorch-win-shared-with-deps-1.8.1+cu102\libtorch\lib\torch_cuda.lib 
...
FLAGS = /DWIN32 /D_WINDOWS /W3 /GR /EHsc /MD /Zi /O2 /Ob1 /DNDEBUG
LINK_FLAGS = /machine:x64 /debug /INCREMENTAL /subsystem:console
LINK_LIBRARIES = D:\Lib\libtorch-win-shared-with-deps-1.8.1+cu102\libtorch\lib\c10.lib
```

But with 1.9.0-rc1 it looks like this:

```
# Link the executable TestProject.exe
build TestProject.exe: CXX_EXECUTABLE_LINKER__TestProject_RelWithDebInfo CMakeFiles\TestProject.dir\LibTorchTestApp.cpp.obj | torch-NOTFOUND
...
FLAGS = /DWIN32 /D_WINDOWS /W3 /GR /EHsc /MD /Zi /O2 /Ob1 /DNDEBUG
LINK_FLAGS = /machine:x64 /debug /INCREMENTAL /subsystem:console
LINK_LIBRARIES = torch-NOTFOUND  -Wl,--no-as-needed,"D:/Lib/libtorch-win-shared-with-deps-1.9.0-rc1/libtorch/lib/libtorch_cpu.so" -Wl,--as-needed  c10-NOTFOUND  -Wl,--no-as-needed,"D:/Lib/libtorch-win-shared-with-deps-1.9.0-rc1/libtorch/lib/libtorch.so"
```

The `libtorch/lib/libtorch_cpu.so` is shockingly strange as it does not event exist on Windows.

author: skyline75489
text: OK this is false alarm. I'm targeting the wrong library. The library itself is a Linux one.
